records.ety: [F. diamtre, L. diametros, fr. Gr. <?/; dia` through + <?/ measure. See Meter.]

defs: Any right line passing through the center of a figure or body, as a circle, conic section, sphere, cube, etc., and terminated by the opposite boundaries; a straight line which bisects a system of parallel chords drawn in a curve.
The length of a straight line through the center of an object from side to side; width; thickness; as, the diameter of a tree or rock.
notes: ☞ In an elongated object the diameter is usually taken at right angles to the longer axis.

defs: The distance through the lower part of the shaft of a column, used as a standard measure for all parts of the order. See Module.
